abstract: Neuroscience has historically served as a foundational catalyst for breakthroughs in artificial intelligence. We argue that further accelerating AI development requires a dedicated investment in NeuroAI—a research synthesis at the intersection of biological and artificial intelligence. Central to this mission is the "embodied Turing test," which challenges artificial agents to interact with the physical world with the same sensorimotor agility and fluidity as biological organisms. By shifting the benchmark from high-level cognitive tasks like chess or natural language processing toward the fundamental behaviors shared by all animals, we refocus the field on capabilities refined over 500 million years of evolution. Developing models capable of passing this embodied Turing test will provide a transformative roadmap for the next generation of artificial intelligence.
